name: Las Doncellas del Hoyal (PRC-P-19) — circular por la Laguna Corazón
region_intro: Jogging around Guardo offers access to diverse natural environments within the Montaña Palentina region of Spain. The area features riverine paths along the Carrión River, extensive pine and beech forests, and open fields. Runners can explore trails ranging from urban green spaces like Parque Fuentes Carrionas to more challenging routes in the surrounding mountainous terrain, including significant peaks and scenic lagoons. This varied landscape provides a range of running experiences for different fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Guardo The most popular jogging route is Las Doncellas del Hoyal (PRC-P-19) — circular por la Laguna Corazón, a 6.6 miles (10.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 24 minutes to complete, featuring the distinctive heart-shaped Corazón de Muñeca Lagoon. Another top favourite among local runners is Roblón de El Monasterio Trail, a difficult 10.0 miles (16.0 km) path that winds through dense forest landscapes. Local runners also love the Cueto del Asno – Peña Mayor loop from Guardo Apeadero, an 11.8 miles (19.0 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 48 minutes. Jogging around Guardo is defined by riverine paths, native pine forests, and mountainous terrain, with options for different ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 70 runners have used komoot to explore Guardo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Guardo?
A: There are over 10 dedicated running routes around Guardo, offering a variety of terrains and distances within the Montaña Palentina region. These include paths along the Carrión River, through pine and beech forests, and across open fields, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Guardo?
A: While many routes in the Montaña Palentina area are moderate to difficult, beginners can find suitable options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120336118/las-doncellas-del-hoyal-prc-p-19-circular-por-la-laguna-corazon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Doncellas del Hoyal (PRC-P-19) — circular por la Laguna Corazón&lt;/a&gt; route is a moderate 6.6-mile trail that offers beautiful scenery without extreme elevation, making it a good choice for those looking for a less strenuous run. Additionally, the urban &lt;strong&gt;Parque Fuentes Carrionas&lt;/strong&gt; provides extensive garden areas and tree-lined paths ideal for a leisurely jog.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while running near Guardo?
A: Jogging around Guardo offers incredibly diverse landscapes. You'll encounter riverine paths along the Carrión River, extensive native pine and beech forests, and open fields. The region also provides access to the Montaña Palentina Natural Park, featuring impressive mountainous terrain, significant peaks like Pico del Fraile, and scenic water bodies such as the heart-shaped Corazón de Muñeca Lagoon.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Guardo?
A: Yes, several circular running routes are available. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120336118/las-doncellas-del-hoyal-prc-p-19-circular-por-la-laguna-corazon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Doncellas del Hoyal (PRC-P-19) — circular por la Laguna Corazón&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 6.6-mile loop featuring the distinctive lagoon. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31875937" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueto del Asno – Peña Mayor loop from Guardo Apeadero&lt;/a&gt;, an 11.8-mile trail through mountainous terrain.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks I can see along the running trails?
A: Many running trails offer stunning sights. You can pass by the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5200388" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roblón of El Monasterio&lt;/a&gt;, a significant natural monument. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120336118/las-doncellas-del-hoyal-prc-p-19-circular-por-la-laguna-corazon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Doncellas del Hoyal route&lt;/a&gt; offers spectacular views of the Palencia Mountain and the distinctive Corazón de Muñeca Lagoon. Some routes also provide panoramic views from peaks like Pico del Fraile.

Q: Are there any running routes suitable for families with children?
A: For families, the urban &lt;strong&gt;Parque Fuentes Carrionas&lt;/strong&gt; is an excellent choice, offering flat, extensive garden areas and tree-lined paths along the Carrión River, perfect for a relaxed jog or stroll. While many trails in the natural park are more challenging, shorter sections or less strenuous paths within the park can also be explored, depending on the family's fitness level.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Guardo?
A: The natural environment around Guardo, including many trails within the Montaña Palentina Natural Park, is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and to check specific park regulations or trail signage for any restrictions. The diverse terrain offers plenty of opportunities for you and your canine companion to enjoy the outdoors.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Guardo?
A: The best time for jogging in Guardo is typically during spring and autumn when the weather is mild and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Spring brings blooming flora, while autumn offers beautiful fall colors in the forests. Summer can be pleasant, especially in the mornings or evenings, but some higher-altitude routes might be more exposed to the sun. Winter jogging is possible, but trails in the mountainous areas may be affected by snow and ice, requiring appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer great viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. The Montaña Palentina region is known for its spectacular panoramic views. Routes that ascend to higher elevations, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38146275" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Fraile (2,001 m) loop from Pinar de Velilla&lt;/a&gt;, will reward you with breathtaking vistas of the surrounding mountains and valleys. The Corazón de Muñeca Lagoon also provides scenic views of the Palencia Mountain.

Q: Are there any running routes that follow the Carrión River?
A: Yes, the Carrión River is a prominent feature in Guardo's landscape, and several running paths follow its course. The urban &lt;strong&gt;Parque Fuentes Carrionas&lt;/strong&gt; is situated on the banks of the Carrión River, offering refreshing paths. For a longer run, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20159267" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guardo – Carrión River in Guardo loop from Guardo Apeadero&lt;/a&gt; is a 9.1-mile trail that allows you to experience the riverine environment.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Guardo?
A: Runners frequently praise the diverse natural beauty and varied terrain around Guardo. They appreciate the mix of riverine paths, dense forests, and mountainous trails that offer different challenges and scenic rewards. The tranquility of the Montaña Palentina Natural Park and the opportunity to connect with nature are often highlighted as key attractions.

Q: Are there any challenging trail running options near Guardo?
A: For experienced trail runners seeking a challenge, Guardo offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120284844/roblon-de-el-monasterio-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roblón de El Monasterio Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 10-mile path winding through dense forest. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38146275" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Fraile (2,001 m) loop from Pinar de Velilla&lt;/a&gt;, which involves significant elevation gain over its 9.9-mile distance.
tips: Beautiful and peculiar heart shape that this lake has, located on the "Las Doncellas del Hoyal" path, with spectacular views of the Palencia Mountain.

waypoints.name: Corazón de Muñeca Lagoon and Guardo
